Author: Marcus Schack <work@marcus-sa.dev>
Date:   Fri Mar 6 16:43:11 2026 +0700

    Workspace owner automatically assigned from authenticated user (#109)
    
    ## Summary
    
    Logged-in users are now automatically set as workspace owners during
    creation. The workspace creation endpoint no longer requires owner
    details in the request—it retrieves the authenticated user's session and
    creates a member_of relationship linking their existing person record to
    the new workspace.
    
    ## Changes
    
    - Remove `ownerDisplayName` and `ownerEmail` from
    `CreateWorkspaceRequest` contract
    - Update workspace creation handler to extract authenticated user via
    `deps.auth.api.getSession()`
    - Create `member_of` edge between user's person record and workspace
    (instead of creating new person)
    - Simplify workspace creation form to require only workspace name (and
    optional description)
    - Add `createTestUser()` helper to smoke test kit for session-based
    authentication
    - Update all 10 smoke tests to authenticate before workspace creation
    and pass auth cookies to API calls
    
    ## Motivation
    
    User details are now available at login time, making them unnecessary as
    form inputs during workspace creation. This simplifies onboarding and
    ensures the workspace owner is always the authenticated user.
    
    🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

```
"The workspace is described as: "${context.workspaceDescription}"",
"Do not ask what the business is — focus on discovering projects and product areas within it."
```

So when the user created a workspace called "DabDash" with a description, the onboarding prompt tells the agent to "focus on discovering projects and product areas within it." 

The user then sent a message with "DASHBOARD" as a heading followed by a description. The agent should interpret "Dashboard" as a **project** within the DabDash workspace, with the bullet points as features/tasks. Looking at the PM agent's classification rules, a project is a named product or top-level workstream with proper nouns and building/launching language, while features are capabilities that support or enable a project—so "Dashboard" should definitely be classified as a project, not a feature.

The fix is to update the onboarding prompt so the agent understands that the workspace itself is the business, the user's descriptions are projects (product areas), and any nested items are features or tasks within those projects. I should also check how the onboarding state is being handled to make sure the agent doesn't accidentally create a duplicate project with the workspace name.

Perfect! I now have a comprehensive understanding of the test patterns and the fixes. Let me create a summary document of my findings:

## Exploration Summary: Smoke Tests, Eval Patterns, and Workspace Classification Fix

### Key Findings

#### 1. **Smoke Test Infrastructure** (`/Users/marcus/Git/brain/tests/smoke/smoke-test-kit.ts`)
- **Setup pattern**: `setupSmokeSuite(suiteName)` creates isolated test namespace/database, boots app server on unique port, applies full schema, and cleans up after tests
- **Test user creation**: `createTestUser(baseUrl, suffix)` signs up via email, returns auth headers with session cookies
- **Chat message flow**: 
  - POST to `/api/chat/messages` with workspace/conversation IDs and message text

- **extraction.eval.ts**: 425 lines - Tests entity extraction quality with 13+ specialized scorers (entity-precision, entity-recall, no-phantom-persons, evidence-grounded, forbidden-kinds, category-accuracy, priority-accuracy, etc.)
- **suggestions.eval.ts**: 158 lines - Tests onboarding suggestion quality against golden cases
- **chat-agent.eval.ts**: 238 lines - Tests chat agent behavioral correctness (tool invocation, tool selection, clarification avoidance)
- **analytics.eval.ts**: Tests analytics agent behavior

**Key characteristics:**
- Calls real LLM models via OpenRouter (EXTRACTION_MODEL, CHAT_AGENT_MODEL, etc.)
- Uses `evalite` framework with custom scorer functions (`createScorer`)
- Stores results in SQLite (`eval-results/evalite.db`)
- Requires env vars: `OPENROUTER_API_KEY`, `EXTRACTION_MODEL`, `CHAT_AGENT_MODEL`
- Uses `setupEvalRuntime` to create isolated SurrealDB namespace/database for each eval run
- Runs with: `bun run eval` or `bun run eval:watch`

### 2. **Smoke Tests** (in `/tests/smoke/`) - For End-to-End Integration
- **onboarding.test.ts**: 130 lines - Tests full onboarding flow with real HTTP calls
- **extraction-quality.test.ts**: 164 lines - Tests extraction correctness (filters placeholders, avoids unresolved persons)
- Other smoke tests for specific features

- Uses Bun's native test framework (`bun:test`)
- Makes actual HTTP calls to running server
- Asserts on database state directly via SurrealDB queries
- Creates isolated test namespaces/databases via `setupSmokeSuite`
- Runs with: `bun test --env-file=.env tests/smoke/`

# Plan: Smoke test for workspace-project hierarchy classification

## Context

During onboarding, when a workspace "DabDash" has a description and the user sends structured content like:
```
DASHBOARD
Your business at a glance.
Real-time order count and revenue stats
Low stock alerts...
```

The agent incorrectly creates "DabDash" as a project and "Dashboard" as a feature. The fix (already applied in this session) teaches the system prompt that workspace name ≠ project and structured headings from the user are projects, not features.

We need a smoke test to verify the fix prevents this misclassification.

## Approach: Add test case to existing `workspace-description.test.ts`

This file already tests workspace+description onboarding flows and has the exact infrastructure needed (workspace creation with description, chat message sending, DB entity verification).

### New test case: "classifies user-described heading as project, not workspace name"

**File:** `tests/smoke/workspace-description.test.ts`

**Test flow:**
1. Create workspace named "DabDash" with description "Cannabis delivery storefront platform"
2. Bootstrap to get conversation
3. Send structured message:
   ```
   DASHBOARD

   Your business at a glance.

   Real-time order count and revenue stats
   Low stock alerts with direct links to inventory
   Onboarding checklist for new stores
   ```
4. Collect SSE events (wait for completion)
5. Query DB and assert:
   - **No project named "DabDash"** exists (workspace name must not become a project)
   - **A project named "Dashboard"** exists (the heading should be classified as a project)

### Key assertions (DB queries)
```typescript
// Query all projects in this workspace
const [projects] = await surreal.query(
  "SELECT id, name FROM project WHERE id IN (SELECT VALUE out FROM has_project WHERE `in` = $workspace);",
  { workspace: workspaceRecord },
);

// Workspace name must NOT appear as a project
expect(projects.some(p => p.name === "DabDash")).toBe(false);

// "Dashboard" should be created as a project
expect(projects.some(p => p.name === "Dashboard")).toBe(true);
```

### Timeout
300s (matches existing chat-flow test cases that call real LLMs).

## Verification
Run: `bun test --env-file=.env tests/smoke/workspace-description.test.ts`

export async function buildPmSystemPrompt(input: {
  surreal: Surreal;
  workspaceRecord: RecordId<"workspace", string>;
}): Promise<string> {
  const [workspace, projects, observations, suggestions] = await Promise.all([
    input.surreal.select<{ name: string }>(input.workspaceRecord),
    listWorkspaceProjectSummaries({
      surreal: input.surreal,
      workspaceRecord: input.workspaceRecord,
      limit: 20,
    }),
    listWorkspaceOpenObservations({
      surreal: input.surreal,
      workspaceRecord: input.workspaceRecord,
      limit: 20,
    }),
    listWorkspacePendingSuggestions({
      surreal: input.surreal,
      workspaceRecord: input.workspaceRecord,
      limit: 20,
    }),
  ]);

  return [
    "You are the Product Manager subagent.",
    "You are the single authority on tasks, features, projects, and project status.",
    "",
    "## Architecture: Graph as Communication Bus",
    "All agent-to-agent communication happens through the knowledge graph.",
    "Never rely on handoff text from other agents; read context from graph tools.",
    "Other agents (Research, Engineering, Marketing, etc.) write to the same graph.",
    "You may discover entities and observations created by other agents when querying.",
    "Your observations are visible to all agents in subsequent invocations.",
    "",
    "## Entity Kind Classification",
    ...(workspace?.name ? [`The workspace "${workspace.name}" is the business/brand — NEVER create a project with the same name as the workspace.`] : []),
    "- Project: named product area, module, or workstream within the workspace (e.g. Dashboard, Inventory, Orders)",
    "- Feature: capability/requirement within a project (supports, provides, enables, needs to handle)",
    "- Task: concrete executable work with action verb (implement, build, fix, migrate, deploy, test)",
    "",
    "## When to Create Directly vs Suggest",
    "- Direct creation (create_work_item): DEFAULT for clearly described items — headings with descriptions, explicit feature/task lists, onboarding entity seeding, and any item the user explicitly names. You MUST call create_work_item for each item before generating your final output.",
    "- Suggestions (suggest_work_items): ONLY when items are vague/uncertain, or you need duplicate analysis against many existing entities. Never use suggest_work_items as a substitute for creating clearly described items.",
    "",
    "## Category Classification (tasks only)",
    "engineering, research, marketing, operations, design, sales.",
    "Classify by what the entity affects, not where it came up.",
    "",
    "## Priority Classification (tasks only)",
    "critical: urgent/ASAP/blocking/P0, high: important/soon/P1, low: nice-to-have/backlog/P3, medium: default.",
